title: Conjecture 1.1 (Andersen)
statement_text: Every properly edge-coloured $K_n$ has a rainbow path of length $n - 2$.
context_text: Andersen conjectured that every properly edge-coloured complete graph contains a rainbow path of length $n-2$. This is part of a broader belief that proper edge-colourings of $K_n$ contain long rainbow substructures, motivated in part by the (disproved) Hahn Conjecture about rainbow Hamiltonian paths.
paper_contribution: Proves that every properly edge-coloured $K_n$ contains a rainbow cycle of length at least $n - 24n^{3/4}$, establishing an approximate version of Andersen's conjecture and improving all earlier bounds.
paper_title: Random subgraphs of properly edge-coloured complete graphs and long rainbow cycles

title: Akbari–Etesami–Mahini–Mahmoody Question on rainbow cycles
statement_text: Does every $1$-factorization of $K_n$ have a rainbow cycle of length at least $n - 2$?
context_text: Akbari, Etesami, Mahini, and Mahmoody also conjectured that every 1-factorization of $K_n$ contains a Hamiltonian cycle with at least $n-2$ different colours, and separately asked whether a rainbow cycle of length at least $n-2$ always exists. These questions are mentioned alongside Andersen's conjecture as central open problems in the study of rainbow subgraphs.
paper_contribution: Proves that every properly edge-coloured $K_n$ (hence every 1-factorization) contains a rainbow cycle of length at least $n - 24n^{3/4}$, making significant progress toward the $n-2$ bound.
notes: Stated in prose in the introduction without a labelled theorem environment; attributed to reference [1].
paper_title: Random subgraphs of properly edge-coloured complete graphs and long rainbow cycles

title: Open Problem: error term for longest rainbow cycle
statement_text: Determine the correct order of the error term in the maximum length of a rainbow cycle guaranteed in every properly edge-coloured $K_n$; currently the deficit is known to lie between $-1$ and $-O(n^{3/4})$.
context_text: Theorem 1.2 establishes a rainbow cycle of length at least $n - 24n^{3/4}$, while Andersen's Conjecture 1.1 posits a rainbow path of length $n-2$. The authors note that the constant $24$ in front of $n^{3/4}$ is not optimised, and leave as an open problem the task of pinning down the true order of the error term.
notes: Stated in prose immediately after Theorem 1.2: 'leaving as an open problem to pin down the correct order of the error term (currently between −1 and −O(n^{3/4}))'.
paper_title: Random subgraphs of properly edge-coloured complete graphs and long rainbow cycles
